growth_records.cite: Lim et al. Biohybrid CO2 electrolysis for the direct synthesis of polyesters from CO2. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2221438120. [PMC10083616]
growth_records.snippet: This system produced 1.38 g PHB with a PHB content of 83.0% of DCW at 120 h. The average PHB productivity during 120 h of operation was 11.5 mg h-1... The initial OD600 was 0.9, which corresponded to an initial DCW of 0.2 g DCW L-1... forma
growth_records.notes: C. necator H16, formate as sole carbon source (from CO2 electrolysis), fed-batch 120 h, 30°C, air-purged (aerobic). GROWTH RATE: non-PHB biomass increased 0.40 g -> 0.56 g over first 24 h; mu = ln(0.56/0.40)/24 = ln(1.4)/24 = 0.3365/24 = 0.0140/h; td = ln2/0.0140 = 49.4 h (growth is formate-limited/near-stationary, PHB-accumulating). (OD-based apparent mu incl. PHB = ln(2.7/0.9)/48 = 0.023/h.) FORMATE UPTAKE: electrolyzer produces formate at 262 mg/h into 2 L; concentration held 0.26-1.0 g/L so consumption ~= supply = 262 mg/h volumetric. Total DCW at 120 h = 1.38 g PHB / 0.83 = 1.66 g in 2 L (0.83 g/L). Specific qFormate = 262 mg/h / 1.66 gDCW = 158 mg/gDCW/h; /46.03 g/mol = 3.42 mmol/gDCW/h. PHB: reported volumetric productivity 11.5 mg/h; specific = 11.5/1.66 = 6.9 mg/gDCW/h; /86.09 g/mol (C4H6O2 monomer) = 0.080 mmol/gDCW/h. YIELD: total formate supplied = 262 mg/h x 120 h = 31.44 g; Y(PHB/formate) = 1.38/31.44 = 0.044 g/g (approx; assumes near-complete formate consumption). Specific rates are approximate (biomass changes over run; used 120 h total DCW) -> confidence medium. pH not stated (catholyte 30 g/L KH2PO4 + 4 g/L K2HPO4 buffered). Seed culture (LB, 30°C, 250 rpm) reported without a growth rate, not tabulated.

growth_records.snippet: During the 18 d of operation, the electrochemical production of formate was stably conducted... and PHB was continually produced to a total of 1.45 g... during the continuous mode, the PHB contents were maintained within a range of 30~40 wt
growth_records.notes: Distinct condition: steady-state/semi-continuous biohybrid run (Fig. 4), 18 days, 30°C, aerobic. Cells and medium exchanged manually every 24 h to maintain constant cell density and constant formate/PHB concentrations ('similar to a chemostat'); classified culture_mode=chemostat but NO dilution rate reported and exchange is manual/semi-continuous, so growth_rate not derivable (left null). Formate supply 262 mg/h (reported). PHB total 1.45 g cumulative; steady-state PHB content 30-40 wt% (up to 53.7 wt%). No specific mu/qS/qP computable from reported data at constant cell density -> confidence low. Second record for same paper (distinct operating mode from the 120 h fed-batch record).

growth_records.cite: Dronsella et al. One-carbon fixation via the synthetic reductive glycine pathway exceeds yield of the Calvin cycle. Nature Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41564-025-01941-9. [PMC11879842]
growth_records.snippet: we obtained biomass yields of 3.88 +/- 0.19 g CDW mol-1 for the CBB cycle strain and 4.52 +/- 0.17 g CDW mol-1 for the rGlyP strain ... in bioreactors in chemostat mode at a dilution rate of 0.05 h-1 (14 h doubling time).
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: at steady state mu = D = 0.05 h^-1 (td = ln2/0.05 = 13.86 h, reported as ~14 h). Measured biomass yield on formate Yxs = 3.88 g CDW/mol formate (n=9 steady-state samples). Formate is sole carbon+energy source. Formate uptake qS derived: qS = mu/Yxs; convert Yxs to gCDW per mmol = 3.88/1000 = 0.00388 gCDW/mmol; qS = 0.05/0.00388 = 12.89 mmol formate/gCDW/h. No residual formate detected by HPLC (all consumed). No organic-acid secretion detected (HPLC screened lactate/acetate/propionate/isobutyrate/butyrate).

growth_records.snippet: we obtained biomass yields of 3.88 +/- 0.19 g CDW mol-1 for the CBB cycle strain and 4.52 +/- 0.17 g CDW mol-1 for the rGlyP strain, showing a 16.6% higher yield compared with the wild type in the same condition.
growth_records.notes: Engineered rGlyP strain (CRG6 delta-dadA6) chemostat steady state: mu = D = 0.05 h^-1 (td ~14 h). Measured Yxs = 4.52 g CDW/mol formate (n=9). Formate sole substrate. qS derived: qS = mu/Yxs = 0.05 / (4.52/1000) = 0.05/0.00452 = 11.06 mmol formate/gCDW/h. rGlyP uses less formate per biomass than CBB (higher yield => lower qS). No residual formate (HPLC below detection); no organic-acid secretion detected.
